2605.06143.title: AI-Generated Images: What Humans and Machines See When They Look at the Same Image
2605.06143.paper_summary: The misuse of generative AI in online disinformation campaigns highlights the urgent need for transparent and explainable detection systems. In this work, we investigate how detectors for AI-generated images can be more effective in providing human-understandable explanations for their predictions. To this end, we develop a suite of detectors with various architectures and fine-tuning strategies, trained on our large-scale photorealistic fake image dataset, AIText2Image, and assess their performance on state-of-the-art text-to-image AI generators. We integrate 16 different explainable AI (XAI) methods into our detection framework, and the visual explanations are comprehensively refined and evaluated through a novel approach that prioritizes human understanding of AI-generated images, using both textual and visual responses collected from a survey of 100 participants. This framework offers insights into visual-language cues in fake image detection and into the clarity of XAI methods from a human perspective, measuring the alignment of XAI outputs with human preferences.
2605.06143.paper_summary_zh: 生成式 AI 在線上虛假資訊活動中的誤用突顯了對透明且可解釋的檢測系統的迫切需求。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們探討了如何使 AI 生成圖像的檢測器在提供人類可理解的預測解釋方面更有效。<lb/>為此，我們開發了一套具有各種架構和微調策略的檢測器，並在我們的大規模真實感虛假圖像數據集 AIText2Image 上進行訓練，評估它們在最先進的文本到圖像 AI 生成器上的性能。<lb/>我們將 16 種不同的可解釋 AI (XAI) 方法整合到我們的檢測框架中，並通過一種新穎的方法全面精煉和評估視覺解釋，該方法優先考慮人類對 AI 生成圖像的理解，使用從 100 名參與者的調查中收集的文本和視覺反應。<lb/>這個框架提供了對虛假圖像檢測中視覺語言線索的洞察，以及從人類角度衡量 XAI 方法清晰度的見解，評估 XAI 輸出與人類偏好的對齊程度。
2605.06143.author: Silvia Poletti et.al.
2605.06143.authors: Silvia Poletti, Justin Ilyes, Marcel Hasenbalg, David Fischinger, Martin Boyer
